characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: The Ageless: According to the manual, because of some unearthly link between him and Tchernobog. He can be killed as any other FPS protagonist can, buttimecertainly won't do it to him - given his listed year of birth as 1847, by the time ofBlood IIhe's pushinga hundred and eighty years old. The Alcoholic: Going by loading interstitials inBlood IIand a few of his own lines from both games, Caleb has something of a drinking problem. Ambiguously Human: He clearly was a human before, but lost his humanity after rising from the dead as an immortalundeadbeing. But exactlywhatkind of immortal undead being he became is up in the air. He's completely different from the brain-hungry zombies he's faced off against, as his body shows no signs of decay and his personality is still intact. However, he also needs to consume hearts (which may bringghoulsto mind) and drink blood to heal himself like avampire. He seems to at least be some kind ofrevenant, being that he came back from the dead in physical form to combat the forces of the Cabal and seek vengeance against Tchernobog. Anti-Hero: To borderlineVillain Protagonistdegrees. Sure, he's fighting against an evil cult, but it's for his own personal goals at worst and he's only barely better than said cult in terms of morality, having no problem with killing innocent bystanders that just happen tobe in his line of fire.Worth noting that Caleb isn't aDefector from Decadenceeither; he was happily a member of the Cabal prior to his rampage, and according to Tchernobog was even a truly loyal disciple of the visceral evil the likes of which the Cabal practiced on the daily, and the only reason he lashes out against the Cabal at all is because its dark master has his friends murdered and casts him aside seemingly for no reason.Nothing about the Cabal's morbid and vile activities so much as perturbed Caleb until it specifically slightedHIM personally. Worth noting that Caleb isn't aDefector from Decadenceeither; he was happily a member o

characters.name: Caleb
characters.description: The Ageless: According to the manual, because of some unearthly link between him and Tchernobog. Worth noting that Caleb isn't aDefector from Decadenceeither; he was happily a member o

characters.name: Ophelia Price
characters.description: Crucified Hero Shot: A grisly one in the first game, where Caleb finds her lashed to the altar she was sacrificed on. Death Is Not Permanent: Dies alongside Caleb in the intro ofBlood, revived during the course ofBlood IIthrough use of the Singularity Generator. Evil Brit: Has a British accent in the second game. Fiery Redhead: Has red hair and the violent tendencies shared amongst her comrades. First-Name Basis: Of the four Chosen, only her last name is made known, and even then only the manual uses it.

characters.name: Ishmael
characters.description: Affably Evil: For an evil cultist, he seems to be quite "diplomatic" in his own way. He acts genuinely clever, polite and jovial, at least towards his fellow Chosen. Any insult towards him just slips off him like a glass sheet, and he's even capable of making fun of his own misery. Badass Longcoat: In the second game, he gets one. Bald of Evil: A member of Tchernabog's cult, and bald. Character Tics: "Hm, yes." Death Is Not Permanent: Dies alongside Caleb in the intro ofBlood, revived during the course ofBlood IIthrough use of the Singularity Generator.

characters.name: Gabriel/Gabriella
characters.description: Death Is Not Permanent: Dies alongside Caleb in the intro ofBlood, revived during the course ofBlood IIthrough use of the Singularity Generator. Gatling Good: She has the Vulcan Cannon as her weapon of choice inThe Nightmare Levels. Gender Flip: Somehow gets brought back to life as a woman inThe Chosen, evidently thanks to the Singularity Generator being just that unpredictable; her only comment is that it's "a long story", and she seems to be fine with it. Mighty Glacier: She's even more powerful than Caleb, at the cost of having the slowest movement speed of the four. Sobriquet Sex Switch: She just went with the feminine form of her name when she popped out of the Singularity as a woman.

characters.name: Cultists
characters.description: Badass Longcoat: In the second game, Zealots have one. Evil Old Folks: Judging by the way theirfaceslook, they could either be wrinkly old people or are simply justGonk'edfrom the influence of the Cabal. The one that gets possessed by Tchernobog in the opening cutscene at least seems to be the case, as he approaches the four Chosen with a limp. The Faceless: Zealots inBlood IIare this, by way of aCool Maskthat completely covers their heads, even the eyes. The game's manual suggests that either they no longer need their eyes to see, or the process of becoming a Zealot causes them toGo Mad from the Revelationand become afraid to seeanything. Facial Horror: Something isvery wrongwith the cultists' faces in the first game. Glass Cannon: They can deal a lot of damage thanks to a varied arsenal, but once well-placed double barrel shot kills them instantly, and only one flare burns their robes.

characters.name: Axe Zombies
characters.description: Close-Range Combatant: Their only weapon is an axe, so they have to come close to Caleb to kill him. The Goomba: Axe Zombies may be tougher than Cultists, but they can be taken down easier as they only run and hit with their axe. Not Quite Dead: If damage past a certain threshold, but not enough for aOne-Hit Kill, is dealt to them, they'll just be knocked to the ground for a few moments before standing up again.

characters.name: Choking Hands
characters.description: Flipping the Bird: When they spot you inThe Chosen, they will gleefully stop, "stand" on their wrists and give you the one-finger salute before giving chase. The Fourth Wall Will Not Protect You: When it's stuck to you, it's actually renderedoverthe HUD, coming as close as it can to actually reaching out of the screen. Helium Speech: They're somehow able to yell "I'll swallow your soul!" and grumble inAngrishin a silly, high-pitched voice. Interface Screw: Causes the screen to darken the longer they're latched on, simulating asphyxiation. Personal Space Invader: They attack by getting close and then wrapping their fingers around Caleb's neck to choke the life out of him.

characters.name: Spiders
characters.description: Interface Screw:Red spidersmake the screen blurry and distorted, while also messing with the health and ammo counters in the original game due to a glitch (Fresh Supplyfixes that part).Green spiders make the screen flicker for a fraction of a second. Red spidersmake the screen blurry and distorted, while also messing with the health and ammo counters in the original game due to a glitch (Fresh Supplyfixes that part). Green spiders make the screen flicker for a fraction of a second. Kill It with Water: Spiders die instantly if submerged in any liquid. Palette Swap: Red and green spiders.

characters.name: Bloated Butchers / Fast Zombies / Puke Zombies
characters.description: Achilles' Heel: The Bloated Butchers are also severely allergic to fire. Alliterative Name:BloatedButcher. Brutal Butcher: They are fat zombies who throw meat cleavers at you as their primary attack, and are often found in blood-soaked areas with table saws and corpses chained up like meat. Dead Weight: They aremore resilientand versatile than regular Axe Zombies, having two ranged attacks, but also a lot slower andcan be set on fire extremely easilywith a quick spray of theAerosol Flamethrower. Fat Bastard: They don't call them "bloated" for nothin'.

characters.name: Bone Eels
characters.description: Artificial Stupidity: They often jump out of water despite immediately dying if they come out.

characters.name: Gargoyles
characters.description: Achilles' Heel: The Stone Gargoyle is extremely resistant to physical damage and fire, but weak to "magic" weapons like the Tesla cannon and voodoo doll. Ballistic Bone: They fling them at you for a ranged attack. Eye Beams: Stone gargoyles have them, and they deal spirit armor damage. Hard-Mode Mook: Inverted. On the easier modes, the Stone Gargoyle reappears as a mook regularly. Kill It with Water: Stone Gargoyles die instantly if submerged in any liquid.

characters.name: Gill Beasts
characters.description: Close-Range Combatant: As they have no long range attacks, they have to get close to Caleb to attack him. Fiendish Fish: They look like giant lanternfishes with a human body, and they are vile opponents. Fish People: Amphibious sea monsters that are very fast and dangerous underwater, but become somewhat slow bipedal walkers on land. Lightning Bruiser: While they are slow on land, they swim extremely fast in water, and in both cases, they are tough and deal a lot of damage.

characters.name: Hell Hounds
characters.description: Achilles' Heel: For whatever reason, the Tesla Cannon makes short work of them. Hard-Mode Mook: In the easier difficulties you only fight Cerberus, the Boss version, and that's it. In the harder difficulties, Hell Hounds appear more often. Hellhounds: Multi-headed dogs withfiery breath. Kill It with Water: Dies instantly if submerged in any liquid.Fresh SupplyandRefreshed Supplyeven grant each an achievement for managing to dispatch a Hellhound by dunking it in the water. Super Drowning Skills: They die instantly when submerged in water, likely from snuffing out their vital fire abilities.

characters.name: Phantasms
characters.description: Evil Laugh: They very,veryrarely let out sinister cackles every once in a while, likely when they're idle. Ghastly Ghost: They look like a corpse in an advanced decaying state. The Grim Reaper: Phantasms are essentially miniature Grim Reapers that are only vulnerable when they become corporeal. They oftenstartle playersby ambushing them with their persistent screaming and creepy smile. Screaming Warrior: Theyconstantlyemit unearthly, bloodcurdling shrieks as they move in for the kill with their scythes. Sinister Scythe: They use it either to directly hit Caleb, or to shoot projectile skulls.

characters.name: Bone Leech / Soul Drudge / Drudge Lord / Drudge Priest
characters.description: And I Must Scream: The Soul Drudges are living humans whose bodies are controlled by Bone Leeches but who are still conscious and aware of what they're doing. The trope name is taken quite literally as the leech forces its host to sew its mouth shut tokeepit from screaming in horror at what it's being forced to do. Body Horror: The Soul Drudges and their advanced forms exhibit various levels of this. Interface Screw: Causes the screen to darken the longer they're latched on, simulating asphyxiation. Magikarp Power: The bone leech is fairly squishy and can be dealt with quickly if you know its location. The final stage of it infesting its host however is a floating monstrosity which hurls fireballs and lightning like there's no tomorrow and doesn't even flinch from taking artillery fire right in its "face". Mook Maker: The Drudge Priest sometimes spawns more Leeches as an attack, especially if you get close or attack the host's belly.

characters.name: Thief
characters.description: Interface Screw:Makes the screen blurry and distorted, while also messing with the health and ammo counters in the original game due to a glitch (Fresh Supplyfixes that part).

characters.name: Cheogh
characters.description: Achilles' Heel: For whatever reason, the Tesla Cannon makes short work of him. Boss in Mook Clothing: After being the tough boss ofThe Way of All Flesh, he reappears in following episodes among other enemies, and he's still as tough as he was in his original fight. First-Person Dying Perspective:At the end of Episode 1, right at the end of the cutscene, Caleb burns Ophelia's body, and then picks his Shotgun, pointing towards the fallen gargoyle. With the Shotgun in hand, Caleb points to the fallen gargoyle, then follows that with a switch towards Cheogh's POV. The cutscene ends as Caleb pulls the trigger, the ending implied that Cheogh's head has been blown up. Lightning Bruiser: Gargoyles are already fast, tough, and strong, but Cheogh is bigger, faster, and stronger than them. He even lacks theirWeaksauce Weaknessto water. The Resenter: According to the manual, Cheogh always had it out for Caleb specifically due to how much further he made it up the ranks.

characters.name: Shial, Mother of Spiders
characters.description: Achilles' Heel: She's incredibly allergic to napalm and fire / explosions in general. Hard-Mode Mook: Appears once or twice in the easier modes. On the harder modes, she appears more often. Mook Maker: Spawns smaller spiders to attack for her, as she can't attack directly. Weaponized Offspring: She spawns regular spiders as her only attack.

characters.name: Cerberus
characters.description: Hellhounds: Multi-headed dog withfiery breath. Helpful Mook: Inthe last level, he can fight against Tchernobog andwinthe game for you. Laser-Guided Karma: Cerberus devoured Ishmael afternailing him with a fireball, and that was the only one of the Chosen's deaths that Caleb witnessed as it happened. And so, upon slaying Cerberus, Caleb slices open his gut andshoves in a load of remote explosives.

characters.name: Tchernobog
characters.description: Batman Gambit: He fully intended for Caleb to return from the grave and seek vengeance, gaining power with each kill he made along the way. The intent was to thentake over Caleb's body and use the new power to take over all realities, though this fails, with Caleb killing him and going on his merry way. Dark Is Not Evil: Tchernobog is technically a neutral force who exists to separate and bind alternate realities. For the most part, Tchernobog's own will overpowers that of his new host, but there have been exceptions, such as his 5th incarnation being a Buddhist high priest who set Tchernobog to turn the Cabal into a religious cult, and the 16th incarnation, a truly evil and bitter man, turned him into the form we see him as in-game. Death Is Not Permanent: Due tothe nature of his reincarnation, it died and come back at least 15 times before Caleb somehow permanently killed him. Dem Bones: He takes the form of a giant, demonic humanoid skeleton in the final battle. Determinator: His method of reincarnation means that he came back from death sixteen times before, but when it came between him and Caleb he didn't stand a chance.

characters.name: Chosen-in-training / Beast
characters.description: Armor-Piercing Attack/Confusion Fu: After transforming into their beast forms, they can perform a stomp attack that ignores Caleb's armor... which is negated while wearing Jump Boots. Fan Disservice: They were already pretty ugly looking like the other cultists, but when they wolf-out, they shed their robes and not only reveal their new monstrous bodies, but also their strangely well-modeled butts.

characters.name: Prophet
characters.description: Elite Mooks: They're a lot tougher than regularCabalCosoldiers, and are usually armed with heavy weapons.

characters.name: Gideon
characters.description: Badass Longcoat: He gets one. Big Bad:Though he's not the final boss. Body Horror:The human half of his arachnoid second form not only inverts his shoulder and elbow joints along with his neck, but his heart has exploded out of his chest and the lower half of his face has been torn off. He wasn't kidding when he called it "something unpleasant". Corrupt Corporate Executive: ConsideringCabalCois essentially a public face for the evil, mystical cult of Tchernobog, he couldn't be anything else. Fate Worse than Death: He's reduced to nothing but his skull after he fights Caleb. It's revealed at the beginning of theNightmare Levelsexpansion that his soul was trapped within it. He isn't any bitter about it, figuring he'll"serve as a musetosome unfortunate playwright"someday, and in the meantime deciding to narrate the continuing adventures of Caleb and company to the player.

characters.name: The Ancient One
characters.description: Omnicidal Maniac: The Ancient One seems to have no greater goal beyond "burn everything". (Well, itthoughtit did, but then realizesmid-speechthat it doesn't know what's supposed to happen afterward... not that this stops it from trying to kill Caleb anyway.) Shapeshifter Guilt Trip: It attempts a variant of this on Caleb using undead copies of the Chosen. Caleb, who knows that it's not actually his friends saying those things, isn't impressed.

characters.name: The Nightmare
characters.description: Ambiguous Situation: It's conceptually identical to the "Lord of All Nightmares" (a demonic canine whose domain is, well, nightmares) fromCryptic Passageand solely targets Caleb, but it's not clear if the Nightmare is the Reality Beta counterpart of the Lord, or the same entity back for revenge.
